tokens.name: Raised
tokens.description:
For slightly raised elevation to give a subtle emphasis to elements on the page background.

Examples: alerts and avatars

tokens.name: Overflow
tokens.description:
For elements that remain fixed, staying in view on scroll, to appear above scrolling content.

Examples: back to top and summary action bar

groups.description: Overlay elevation is used for elements that are temporary and more visually distinct via higher elevation from the surface.

tokens.name: Overlay 100
tokens.description:
For a small overlay elevation on temporary elements that are easily dismissed.

Examples: popovers, dropdown menus, and picker inputs

tokens.name: Overlay 200
tokens.description:
For an overlay elevation that gives more emphasis to larger elements that are used in conjunction with the main page.

Example: flyouts

tokens.name: Overlay 300
tokens.description:
For floating overlays that require clear visual separation. Use for interruptive elements.

Examples: modals and toasts
